MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff William Jellyman.
Dana Burch of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant Frasers Boilers Service Inc.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to advance the trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Conditional opposition filed by Fraser's Boiler Services. The trial date of October 5, 2020 is advanced to September 28, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 2, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 24, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 11, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 25, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 3, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

Off Calendar Jun-14-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Josh S. Sullivan of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant and moving party Ferro Engineering Division of On Marine Services Company, LLC.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party William Jellyman.

RULING: DEFENDANT FERRO ENGINEERING DIVISION OF ON MARINE SERVICES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ferro Engineering Division of On Marine Services Company, LLC, a Delaware Limited Liability Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. Statements made during the deposition of William Jellyman preclude Defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Jennifer Roux of US Legal Support, CSR #11033 (Tel: 415-362-4346). (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-13-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff William Jellyman.

RULING: DEFENDANT DANA COMPANIES, LLC SUED HEREIN AS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES

No other appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dana Companies, LLC's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Not reported. (503/CML)
